title: Classification of Data Structures
description: A hierarchical tree diagram showing the classification of data structures into primitive and non-primitive categories, with their respective subtypes.
relationships: Primitive Data Structures branch into Integer, Float, Character, Boolean, and Pointer
Non-Primitive Data Structures branch into Linear and Non-Linear structures
Linear Data Structures branch into Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, and Queues
Non-Linear Data Structures branch into Trees and Graphs
